growth_records.medium: sand microcosm (4.8% gravimetric water content) contaminated with dibenzofuran (DBF)
growth_records.cite: Moreno-Forero et al. Genome-wide analysis of Sphingomonas wittichii RW1 behaviour during inoculation and growth in contaminated sand. The ISME Journal (2014). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2014.101. [PMC4274413]
growth_records.snippet: Population development of RW1 during the early phase in sand with DBF (12-28 h) was best represented by exponential growth ... with a calculated growth rate of 0.24±0.06 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate (0.24 +/- 0.06 h-1) directly reported in paper, calculated by the authors themselves from the increase of log CFU/g sand over time (12-28 h post-inoculation) in triplicate microcosms (Methods: 'The growth rate in soil was calculated from the increase of the log CFU over time'). doubling_time_h derived here as ln2/0.24 = 2.89 h for completeness. Incubation temperature not explicitly stated for the sand microcosm growth assay (sand was stored at room temperature 18-22C prior to use, but this is not confirmed as the incubation temperature during the growth experiment itself, so temperature_C left null). [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, pH]

growth_records.medium: DSM457 phosphate-buffered mineral medium with DBF crystals
growth_records.cite: Moreno-Forero et al. Genome-wide analysis of Sphingomonas wittichii RW1 behaviour during inoculation and growth in contaminated sand. The ISME Journal (2014). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2014.101. [PMC4274413]
growth_records.snippet: This is similar to the growth rate observed in liquid cultures with DBF crystals (0.23±0.01 h-1).
growth_records.notes: Growth rate (0.23 +/- 0.01 h-1) for RW1 growing in liquid batch culture on DBF crystals (DSM457 mineral medium, 30C, 180 rpm rotary shaking, aerobic) directly reported in the paper, determined by the same CFU-based method as the sand cultures. doubling_time_h derived here as ln2/0.23 = 3.01 h for completeness. [metadata completed from paper Methods: pH]

growth_records.medium: DSM457-based minimal medium (MM) with 5 mM phenanthrene (PHE)
growth_records.cite: Edith Coronado et al. Engineered membrane superchannel improves bioremediation potential of dioxin-degrading bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2012.00300. [PMC3425912]
growth_records.snippet: Maximum specific growth rates of RW1 in batch culture were different on PHE, SAL, or DBF, with 0.24 +/- 0.09, 0.16 +/- 0.01, and 0.20 +/- 0.03 h-1, respectively. ... 5 mM PHE ... incubated at 30C with rotary shaking at 180 rpm.
growth_records.notes: Maximum specific growth rate on phenanthrene (PHE) = 0.24 +/- 0.09 h-1 (experimental, Figure 3). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.24 = 2.89 h. Base medium is DSM457-based MM amended with 5 mM PHE as carbon source; full mineral composition not given in text so medium_composition_stated=false.

growth_records.medium: DSM457-based minimal medium (MM) with 5 mM salicylate (SAL)
growth_records.cite: Edith Coronado et al. Engineered membrane superchannel improves bioremediation potential of dioxin-degrading bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2012.00300. [PMC3425912]
growth_records.snippet: Maximum specific growth rates of RW1 in batch culture were different on PHE, SAL, or DBF, with 0.24 +/- 0.09, 0.16 +/- 0.01, and 0.20 +/- 0.03 h-1, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Maximum specific growth rate on salicylate (SAL) = 0.16 +/- 0.01 h-1 (experimental, Figure 3). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.16 = 4.33 h. 5 mM SAL as carbon source; base DSM457-based MM composition not detailed.

growth_records.medium: DSM457-based minimal medium (MM) with dibenzofuran (DBF) crystals
growth_records.cite: Edith Coronado et al. Engineered membrane superchannel improves bioremediation potential of dioxin-degrading bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2012.00300. [PMC3425912]
growth_records.snippet: Maximum specific growth rates of RW1 in batch culture were different on PHE, SAL, or DBF, with 0.24 +/- 0.09, 0.16 +/- 0.01, and 0.20 +/- 0.03 h-1, respectively. ... DBF crystals in a dosage of 5 umol/ml as carbon source.
growth_records.notes: Maximum specific growth rate on dibenzofuran (DBF) = 0.20 +/- 0.03 h-1 (experimental, Figure 3). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.20 = 3.47 h. DBF crystals dosed at 5 umol/ml as carbon source; base DSM457-based MM composition not detailed.

growth_records.medium: DSM457-based minimal medium (MM) with 5 mM phenanthrene (PHE), chemostat
growth_records.cite: Edith Coronado et al. Engineered membrane superchannel improves bioremediation potential of dioxin-degrading bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2012.00300. [PMC3425912]
growth_records.snippet: The flow rate was set to 23.8 ml/h, giving a dilution rate of 0.05 h-1, which corresponds to a doubling time of 14.6 h. ... operated in batch mode at 30C and with a stirring speed of 200 rpm. The pH and pO2 were maintained at 7 and ~90%, re
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state on 5 mM PHE: dilution rate D = 0.05 h-1 (reported), so mu = D = 0.05 h-1. Doubling time 14.6 h reported explicitly (consistent: ln2/0.05 = 13.86 h; authors state 14.6 h). Temperature 30C, pH 7, pO2 ~90% (aerobic), 200 rpm. Streptomycin 50 ug/ml added for contamination control (RW1 naturally Sm-resistant).
